source_units.text_raw:
The Growth Node Zone is intended to accommodate compact, mixed-use development in strategically identified areas of Charlottetown that can support higher density housing, commercial services, and community amenities. These nodes are planned to concentrate growth in walkable, transit-supportive locations, reduce pressure on outlying areas, and create vibrant neighbourhood centres. Development in this zone should promote a mix of residential, retail, office, and institutional uses in mid-rise built forms, supported by high- quality public spaces, active transportation connections, and sustainable infrastructure.
Rezoning from GN is not permitted.
The following uses are permitted in the GN Zone:
Cemetery
Commercial Daycare Centre
Community Care Facility
Community Institutional
Cultural Establishment
Dormitory
Educational Institution (private)
Educational Institution (public)
Fire Station
Funeral Establishment
Government Offices
Hospital
Library
Medical, Health and Dental Office
Place of Worship
Police Station
Public Utility Service Operation
Recreation or Fitness Center (indoors)
Automobile Sales and Services
Automobile Service Station
Cannabis Retail Store
Convenience Store
Drive-thru Business
Eating and Drinking Establishment
Entertainment Establishment
Equipment Sales, Rental Service
Garden Centre
Greenhouse (nursery retail)
Grocery Store
Hostel / Hotel
Laundromat
Office
Outdoor Retail Display
Personal Service Shop
Retail Store
Retail Store with connected Retail Warehouse, Light Manufacturing or assembly facility
Retail Warehouse
Service Repair Establishment
Shopping Centre
Theatre
Transportation Service
Veterinary Services
Warehouse, Storage Facility and/or Distribution Centre
Parking Lot / Structure
Transport Terminal
These uses are permitted so long as the following lot requirements are met:
(a) Lot Area N/A
(b) Frontage min. 12 m
(c) Front Yard Stepback min. 2 m max. 6 m the front yard setback may be greater than the max. for up to 20% of the streetline
(d) Flanking Yard Setback min. 2 m, the flankage yard setback may be greater than the max. for up to 20% of the streetline
(e) Side Yard Setback min. 0 m, 6 m bordering residential zones
(f) Rear Yard Setback min. 12 m when abutting RN, or RM, otherwise 6 m
(g) Coverage max. 60%, 80% if half the parking is located underground
Bonus building height requests shall be considered following Section 2.15.
Flaglots are permitted with no less than 6 m frontage as long as all other site and building requirements are met in Section 23.3.2 for both the flaglot and the main lot.
Permitted encroachments into the setbacks are listed in Table 3.2.
Home occupations are permitted if Section 4.9 requirements are met.
One primary building per lot is permitted.
Attached garages shall not front onto the street frontage unless it provides access to an underground parking garage that does not exceed 25% of the primary building frontage on any streetline to a maximum width of 6 m.
All street-facing facades shall provide at least one horizontal or vertical facade articulation of no less than 300 mm for no less than 30% of the facade area.
Streetwall Stepbacks vary as follows:
(a) For buildings that are 8 storeys or less, streetwalls shall stepback no less than 3 m above the streetwall for no less than 70% of the streetwall.
(b) For buildings that exceed 8 storeys, streetwalls shall stepback no less than 4 m above the streetwall for no less than 70% of the streetwall.
The streetwall height in the GN Zone is 4 storeys. A 5 storey streetwall height may be considered as part of the requests for additional bonus height following Sections 2.15.
Maximum Building Height is set out in Schedule 23.1.
The maximum building dimension is 70 m.
The groundfloor height shall:
(a) Not be less than 4 m high,
(b) Not be less than 3.5 m high if no more than 20% of the floor slab is a stepped slab.
Above the 8th storey, the maximum tower dimension are 40 m x 40 m and no more than 800 m2.
Towers shall be setback no less than 12 m from all side yard or rear yard property lines.
If two towers share the same midrise, the tower separation shall be 24 m.
Parking is not permitted between the street and the building.
The main lobby and elevator core shall exit onto the street-front.
The amenity requirements in Section 3.14 shall be followed.
For multi-unit buildings, each grade-related unit shall include a patio door.
All driveways shall be hard surfaced with asphalt, concrete or unit pavers. Loose gravel or soil is not permitted.
Only one driveway per 20 m of frontage is permitted to a maximum of 2 driveways per lot. Shared driveways are encouraged between neighbouring properties whenever possible.
A minimum 2 m wide hard surfaced walkway shall link the parking lot to the front door of every building.
A minimum 2 m wide hard surfaced walkway shall link the sidewalk to the front door of every building.
If there are no street trees in the bordering public street right-of-way, each development 70 m, Tower 40 m x 40 m and up to 800 m2 max. Plate size shall include no less than 1 tree per 10 m of frontage between the building and the street. Decimal results shall be rounded up.
A 3 m landscape buffer is required for any GN properties that border residential zoned properties in the rear yard. Each landscape buffer shall include:
A 1.8 m high opaque fence along the rear yard
One tree per 6 m of rear yard border
Shrub beds at a rate of 2 m2 per tree consisting of shrubs, annuals or perennials.
All disturbed surfaces shall be seeded, sodded or hydroseeded with grass or meadow mixture or restored to natural condition.
Each grade-related unit must provide no less than 4 m2 of private outdoor amenity space (patio, balcony, rooftop deck, or small yard) to ensure resident comfort and privacy.
Surface parking lots that exceed 24 cars shall include one landscaped island per 15 parking spots. Decimal results shall be rounded up. MOUNT EDWARD RD OAK DR UNIVERSITY AV NASSAU ST MAPLE AV BUCHANAN DR KENSINGTON RD PARKSID E DR LOWER MALPEQUE RD BRACKLEY POINT RD BARDIN CR ST PETERS RD BELVEDERE AV PARKVIEW DR BEACH GROVE RD NORTH RIVER RD CEDAR AV QUEEN ST ASH DR ATLAN TI C RD ALLEN ST TRAF ALGAR ST CAPITAL DR GATES DR BARBOUR CIR MALPEQUE RD SCOTT ST MAPLEWOOD CR BIRCH HILL DR PINE DR MASSEY DR UNDEFINED RAIDER SRD POPE AV NEW LAND CR CORTLAND ST 0 400 800200 m Schedule 23.1 GN Height Precinct - Numbers represent maximum storeys (m)

Development in this zone should promote a mix of residential, retail, office, and institutional uses in mid-rise built forms, supported by high- quality public spaces, active transportation connections, and sustainable infrastructure.
